title: Tom Seay - Best of America by Horseback
h1: Tom Seay - Best of America by Horseback
text: Tom Seay - Best of America by Horseback Americas premier equine travel and trail riding show, Best of America by Horseback. BOABH is hosted by Tom Seay as he travels around the country, showcasing trail riding locations and meeting interesting people along the way. Tom Seay loves Cavallo Horse Hoof Boots as they are all-terrain, durable and secure. With a love for American history, Tom often re-creates historic rides on location, with memorable rides like the Chisholm Trail, Knights of the Golden Horseshoe, Jack Jouette, President Lincoln and many others. Tom lives the cowboy lifestyle. You can even join him and be part of the production of his television show. On epic rides, BOABH trusts Cavallo as part of the required horse equipment, especially if a horse throws a horseshoe. Let Tom Seay tell you about his experience with Cavallos: Tom Seay Reviews Cavallo Horse Hoof Boots and Saddle Pads: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9aO6v77URrs Tom Seay Reviews Cavallo Horse Hoof Boots and Saddle Pads: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W_ZRxVg9EtY

title: Why Barefoot?
text: Why Barefoot Hoof Care? Barefoot Horse Riding Many of us are happy to allow our horses to ‘go barefoot’. We see the benefits of a more natural program. We don’t like pounding nails into the hoof every 6 weeks. Shoeing is expensive and good farriers are hard to find. If we consider pulling the shoes, our horses may seem sore. Maybe you have even tried it and ended up putting the shoes back on. There are many factors that contribute to the long-term success of a barefoot program and it is very different than traditional shoeing. When a farrier comes, he trims your horse flat and level, even and balanced. He removes some dead tissue and makes the hoof look neat and tidy. His job is to make sure the shoe goes on securely and shapes to the trim he’s done, leaving the metal shoe as the only part making contact with the ground. Do you think this metal shoe absorbs the shock and concussion of 1000 pounds in motion? Do you think that the hoof is functioning as it should? Do you think blood is circulating freely? The answers are – not a chance! Click Here to Download the Bare Facts Barefoot Horse - Trims Let’s talk about what happens when we don’t put the shoes on. Proportionately, as a truly miraculous structure, the small hoof supports the weight of a large horse. The walls expand and widen apart to draw the sole flat. When the sole levels downward towards the ground the bone column ca

title: Why does Sandi Simons Recommend Cavallos?
h1: Why Does Sandi Simons Recommend Cavallo Hoof Boots?
text: Why Does Sandi Simons Recommend Cavallo Hoof Boots? Sandi Simons is a mother of five, a wife, a businesswoman, a horse trainer and an International horse presenter! She developed a remarkable training system called Creating Confidence. It’s designed for women who feel they’ve have lost confidence in the saddle for any reason. She’s now helped thousands of women all over the world to regain their confidence to get back in the saddle, whilst enjoying a safe and harmonious relationship with their horses! Sandi Simons’s Students Look to her for Recommendations Sandi finds that the women taking her classes often ask her how best to protect their horse’s hoofs during training, without putting metal shoes on. Why Does Sandi Simons Recommend Cavallos Hoof Boots? This is how Sandi and Carole met! Sandi had decided that Cavallo Hoof Boots were the best on the market, so she reached out to Carole for more information to share with her students. The rest is history! A life-long horsewoman, Sandi’s worked in many aspects of the horse industry in Australia long term. She’s also been involved with some of the biggest equine events around the world. Her insightful, compassionate nature makes her a truly unique and effective coach. Sandi’s motto? Live your dream, love your passion and come and see what it is all about. After all, you’re worth it! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U6sBsSKQjUI

title: Why Horses Can't Go Barefoot - Replay
text: Why Horses Can't Go Barefoot Why Horses Can’t Go Barefoot - Video with Dr. Tomas Teskey, DVM (Insight to Equus) Dr.Tomas Teskey delivers an in-depth look into horse physiology. He explains overall horse care requirements, both physically and psychologically, including the importance of equine dentistry and its effect on horses’ hoofs: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-52eeBPaLew

title: BFB (Big Foot Boot) Draft Size Hoof Boot (sold per single boot)
text: Cavallo BFB (Big Foot Boot) SOLD AS SINGLE BOOTS (Available in Regular Sole, sizes 7-10) The Cavallo BFB (Big Foot Boot) is your go-to hoof boot for draft and other large-hoofed horses. Designed to provide support for everyday riding, trail use, and hoof rehabilitation, the BFB is the perfect choice for your larger-hooved companions. Whether you're new to hoof boots or a seasoned user, the BFB delivers Cavallo’s trusted quality, now in larger sizes. Crafted from durable 1680 Denier industrial-grade nylon, the Cavallo BFB Hoof Boot is built to last. It can be used barefoot or over metal horseshoes, making it ideal for arena riding, trails, turnout, transport, stall rest, or as a "spare tire" for lost shoes. The replaceable Velcro closure ensures a secure fit, while padded leather inside the heel bulb adds extra comfort and protection. Just like all Cavallo boots, the BFB is designed for ease of use: Easy On. Stays On. Easy Off. Cavallo’s BFB Hoof Boot is available in Regular sole width in sizes 7 through 10. Before ordering, please confirm your horse’s size by submitting measuring photos or by following our sizing guide . The versatile front-closure design ensures a snug fit for a wide range of draft and large-breed horses. The BFB is the top-recommended hoof boot for draft breeds, thanks to Cavallo’s sturdy and reliable design, built on one of the largest soles available in the
